project_summary: Converts biomass into battery-grade graphite using a lower-temperature thermochemical process.
school_or_lab: MIT delta v / Martin Trust Center for MIT Entrepreneurship
institution_affiliation_basis: Named in the official MIT delta v 2025 Demo Day program; individual academic affiliations are shown there.
target_market_geography: United States; global battery supply chain
source_locator: Official Demo Day 2025 program, PDF p. 8 (Carbion)
funding_notes: Public-web screen through 2026-07-20 found no disclosed institutional equity round for this exact venture. MIT delta v participation may include equity-free fellowship support; this is not cap-table verification.
founder_score_rationale: Team-level execution signal: The team progressed from a homemade reactor to a 10x larger batch process and commercial discussions. No university prestige or network proxy was scored. Individual contribution is not separately evidenced in the source.
vision_product_rationale: A specific domestic-supply problem is matched to a process innovation for biomass-derived graphite. Score reflects problem clarity, product coherence, and prototype/pilot maturity rather than pedigree.
differentiation_rationale: The claimed lower process temperature, domestic feedstock, and battery-grade output create a potentially differentiated cost/carbon position. Defensibility, freedom to operate, and reproducibility remain to be independently validated.
traction_rationale: Three LOIs and one purchase order under discussion are meaningful pre-revenue signals, but no booked revenue is disclosed. Hackathon selection, accelerator admission, grants, and prizes are not counted as customer traction.
evaluation_scope: Project/team-level public evidence; scores repeat across co-founders and do not isolate individual contribution.
key_evidence: 10x batch capacity increase from 5g to 50g; 3 LOIs; 1 purchase order under discussion; Forestry Business Accelerator.
counter_evidence: No independent material-specification test, gross-margin proof, scaled production run, or signed purchase order was located.
unknowns: Founder ownership and time commitment; graphite qualification results; capex and yield at scale; exact LOI counterparties; cap table.
next_diligence_action: Request third-party graphite test reports, LOI/customer references, scale-up economics, founder role split, and cap-table confirmation.
recommended_reason: Founder Score, Vision & Product, Differentiation above 75; 4 of 4 parameters above 50. Strict > thresholds; no weighted average.

project_summary: AI building engineer that turns commercial-building data into automated fault diagnostics and control actions.
school_or_lab: MIT delta v / Martin Trust Center for MIT Entrepreneurship
institution_affiliation_basis: Named in the official MIT delta v 2025 Demo Day program.
source_locator: Official Demo Day 2025 program, PDF p. 9 (Clima Technologies)
funding_notes: Public-web screen through 2026-07-20 found no disclosed institutional equity round for this exact venture. MIT delta v participation may include equity-free fellowship support; this is not cap-table verification.
founder_score_rationale: Team-level execution signal: The team launched two large-building pilots and secured a channel partnership after extensive customer discovery. No university prestige or network proxy was scored. Individual contribution is not separately evidenced in the source.
vision_product_rationale: The product directly targets energy waste and facilities-team capacity through conversational diagnostics and automation. Score reflects problem clarity, product coherence, and prototype/pilot maturity rather than pedigree.
differentiation_rationale: A building-specific AI copilot with action workflows is differentiated from static dashboards, though integration depth is unverified. Defensibility, freedom to operate, and reproducibility remain to be independently validated.
traction_rationale: Two pilots in buildings above 100,000 square feet and a sustainability-engineering partnership show early market access. Hackathon selection, accelerator admission, grants, and prizes are not counted as customer traction.
evaluation_scope: Project/team-level public evidence; scores repeat across co-founders and do not isolate individual contribution.
key_evidence: 2 Boston office-building pilots; 50+ interviews; partnership with a building-sustainability engineering firm.
counter_evidence: No paid contract value, measured energy savings, deployment duration, or renewal evidence was disclosed.
unknowns: Data integrations; cybersecurity; sales cycle; pilot-to-paid conversion; savings attribution; founder time commitment; cap table.
next_diligence_action: Verify pilot counterparties, measured savings, paid status, integration burden, and repeatable go-to-market motion.
recommended_reason: Founder Score, Vision & Product above 75; 4 of 4 parameters above 50. Strict > thresholds; no weighted average.

project_summary: Uses synthetic user populations to simulate customer behavior and test digital product UX before launch.
school_or_lab: MIT delta v / Martin Trust Center for MIT Entrepreneurship
institution_affiliation_basis: Named in the official MIT delta v 2025 Demo Day program.
source_locator: Official Demo Day 2025 program, PDF p. 10 (Cognify)
funding_notes: Public-web screen through 2026-07-20 found no disclosed institutional equity round for this exact venture. MIT delta v participation may include equity-free fellowship support; this is not cap-table verification.
founder_score_rationale: Team-level execution signal: The team trained behavior-simulation models, launched a private beta, and expanded from design tests to full website journeys. No university prestige or network proxy was scored. Individual contribution is not separately evidenced in the source.
vision_product_rationale: The product offers a clear faster alternative to slow UX research and A/B test cycles. Score reflects problem clarity, product coherence, and prototype/pilot maturity rather than pedigree.
differentiation_rationale: Synthetic-population simulation trained on historical tests may create proprietary performance data, but accuracy methodology is not independently reviewed. Defensibility, freedom to operate, and reproducibility remain to be independently validated.
traction_rationale: Private beta and pilots across e-commerce and software are credible, while commercial terms and retention remain undisclosed. Hackathon selection, accelerator admission, grants, and prizes are not counted as customer traction.
evaluation_scope: Project/team-level public evidence; scores repeat across co-founders and do not isolate individual contribution.
key_evidence: 80%+ self-reported behavior-simulation accuracy; private beta; pilots; feature expansion to user journeys and websites.
counter_evidence: Accuracy is self-reported; no benchmark protocol, paid usage, customer references, or repeat usage metrics were located.
unknowns: Training-data rights; benchmark leakage; customer economics; workflow integration; founder-specific contribution; cap table.
next_diligence_action: Review benchmark design, live customer outcomes versus conventional research, paid status, data moat, and security posture.
recommended_reason: Founder Score, Vision & Product, Differentiation above 75; 4 of 4 parameters above 50. Strict > thresholds; no weighted average.

project_summary: Computer-vision and AI platform for movement analysis, injury-risk prediction, and personalized athletic recovery plans.
sector: Sports tech; digital health; computer vision
school_or_lab: MIT delta v / Martin Trust Center for MIT Entrepreneurship
institution_affiliation_basis: Named in the official MIT delta v 2025 Demo Day program.
source_locator: Official Demo Day 2025 program, PDF p. 11 (Durability)
funding_notes: Public-web screen through 2026-07-20 found no disclosed institutional equity round for this exact venture. MIT delta v participation may include equity-free fellowship support; this is not cap-table verification.
founder_score_rationale: Team-level execution signal: The team completed extensive discovery and put a product in the hands of a small beta group. No university prestige or network proxy was scored. Individual contribution is not separately evidenced in the source.
vision_product_rationale: The product connects movement assessment to prevention and recovery recommendations in a coherent athlete workflow. Score reflects problem clarity, product coherence, and prototype/pilot maturity rather than pedigree.
differentiation_rationale: Computer-vision assessment may improve accessibility, but clinical validity and differentiation from existing movement-analysis tools are unclear. Defensibility, freedom to operate, and reproducibility remain to be independently validated.
traction_rationale: Fifteen beta users and a 250-person waitlist indicate interest but not durable engagement or willingness to pay. Hackathon selection, accelerator admission, grants, and prizes are not counted as customer traction.
evaluation_scope: Project/team-level public evidence; scores repeat across co-founders and do not isolate individual contribution.
key_evidence: 200+ interviews; 15 beta users; 250-person waitlist.
counter_evidence: No injury-outcome validation, paid users, retention, clinician partnership, or regulatory strategy was found.
unknowns: Model validation; liability; target buyer; data privacy; engagement; founder roles; cap table.
next_diligence_action: Request beta cohort retention, movement-model validation, clinician references, pricing tests, and regulatory/liability plan.
recommended_reason: 4 of 4 parameters above 50. Strict > thresholds; no weighted average.

project_summary: Guarantee-first egg-freezing pathway that uses a blood-test model to assess eligibility and price a money-back guarantee.
school_or_lab: MIT delta v / Martin Trust Center for MIT Entrepreneurship
institution_affiliation_basis: Named in the official MIT delta v 2025 Demo Day program.
source_locator: Official Demo Day 2025 program, PDF p. 12 (eggPlan)
funding_notes: Public-web screen through 2026-07-20 found no disclosed institutional equity round for this exact venture. MIT delta v participation may include equity-free fellowship support; this is not cap-table verification.
founder_score_rationale: Team-level execution signal: The team signed an anchor-clinic pilot, deployed a second model version, and moved initial customers into screening. No university prestige or network proxy was scored. Individual contribution is not separately evidenced in the source.
vision_product_rationale: A refund guarantee tied to individualized fertility assessment addresses pricing uncertainty and trust in egg freezing. Score reflects problem clarity, product coherence, and prototype/pilot maturity rather than pedigree.
differentiation_rationale: Underwriting fertility outcomes through a proprietary eligibility model and clinic network is distinctive, but actuarial robustness is unproven. Defensibility, freedom to operate, and reproducibility remain to be independently validated.
traction_rationale: An anchor clinic, reserved capacity, and ten screened customers are concrete early signals; completed paid cycles are not shown. Hackathon selection, accelerator admission, grants, and prizes are not counted as customer traction.
evaluation_scope: Project/team-level public evidence; scores repeat across co-founders and do not isolate individual contribution.
key_evidence: Anchor clinic pilot; eggPlanIQ v2 live; 1,500+ surveys and 120+ interviews; first 10 customers screened.
counter_evidence: No outcome cohort, guarantee-loss assumptions, regulatory/legal analysis, or completed customer revenue was disclosed.
unknowns: Actuarial model; adverse selection; clinic economics; financing reserves; medical/regulatory obligations; cap table.
next_diligence_action: Audit model validation and guarantee economics, confirm clinic agreement and customer payment status, and review legal/regulatory structure.
recommended_reason: Founder Score, Vision & Product, Differentiation above 75; 4 of 4 parameters above 50. Strict > thresholds; no weighted average.

project_summary: At-home saliva sensor and personalized insights for tracking hormones and managing PCOS symptoms.
school_or_lab: MIT delta v / Martin Trust Center for MIT Entrepreneurship
institution_affiliation_basis: Named in the official MIT delta v 2025 Demo Day program; faculty PI was excluded from this founder import.
target_market_geography: Global women’s health; initial Saudi/US networks
source_locator: Official Demo Day 2025 program, PDF p. 14 (Gees)
funding_notes: Public-web screen through 2026-07-20 found no disclosed institutional equity round for this exact venture. MIT delta v participation may include equity-free fellowship support; this is not cap-table verification.
founder_score_rationale: Team-level execution signal: The team combined biosensor development, medical-advisor relationships, and a sizable consumer interest funnel. No university prestige or network proxy was scored. Individual contribution is not separately evidenced in the source.
vision_product_rationale: Frequent at-home hormone feedback could shorten the trial-and-error loop in PCOS lifestyle management. Score reflects problem clarity, product coherence, and prototype/pilot maturity rather than pedigree.
differentiation_rationale: A non-invasive saliva sensor linked to symptom patterns could be differentiated, subject to analytical and clinical validation. Defensibility, freedom to operate, and reproducibility remain to be independently validated.
traction_rationale: More than 500 waitlist registrations and broad social reach show demand interest, not clinical adoption or revenue. Hackathon selection, accelerator admission, grants, and prizes are not counted as customer traction.
evaluation_scope: Project/team-level public evidence; scores repeat across co-founders and do not isolate individual contribution.
key_evidence: 500+ waitlist; 5M+ social reach and 14K interactions; medical-advisor partnerships; support from Saudi health minister.
counter_evidence: No analytical sensitivity/specificity, clinical trial, regulatory pathway, paid conversion, or manufacturing evidence was found.
unknowns: Assay performance; biomarker scope; FDA/other regulation; manufacturing cost; data privacy; cap table.
next_diligence_action: Obtain assay validation data, clinical study design, regulatory plan, prototype manufacturing economics, and waitlist conversion tests.
recommended_reason: Founder Score, Vision & Product, Differentiation above 75; 4 of 4 parameters above 50. Strict > thresholds; no weighted average.

key_evidence: 10 kW HRU built and validated; 5 kW HRU-plus-engine system demonstrated; technoeconomic models completed.

unknowns: Energy efficiency; carrier sourcing/recycling; emissions; maintenance; field reliability; IP; cap table.
next_diligence_action: Review independent test data, catalyst/lifecycle economics, safety certification, fleet pilot plan, and customer LOIs.

project_summary: AI-powered procurement and dispute-management platform for independent restaurants.

institution_affiliation_basis: Named in the official MIT delta v 2025 Demo Day program.
source_locator: Official Demo Day 2025 program, PDF p. 22 (Pancho)
funding_notes: Public-web screen through 2026-07-20 found no disclosed institutional equity round for this exact venture. MIT delta v participation may include equity-free fellowship support; this is not cap-table verification.
founder_score_rationale: Team-level execution signal: The team launched the platform and closed four initial restaurant clients after more than 100 industry interviews. No university prestige or network proxy was scored. Individual contribution is not separately evidenced in the source.
vision_product_rationale: The product targets clear procurement leakage and dispute workflows without requiring formal inventory changes. Score reflects problem clarity, product coherence, and prototype/pilot maturity rather than pedigree.
differentiation_rationale: Centralized ordering plus price/dispute intelligence is useful, but competitive differentiation from restaurant procurement software is moderate. Defensibility, freedom to operate, and reproducibility remain to be independently validated.

evaluation_scope: Project/team-level public evidence; scores repeat across co-founders and do not isolate individual contribution.
key_evidence: 100+ interviews; platform launched; 4 clients; up to 5% food-cost savings reported.
counter_evidence: No customer names, ARR, retention, margin, integration cost, or independently measured savings were found.
unknowns: Pricing; sales cycle; data integrations; client retention; gross margin; founder roles; cap table.
next_diligence_action: Speak with all four clients, verify savings and payment, inspect retention/usage, and map competitive displacement.
recommended_reason: Founder Score, Vision & Product, Traction above 75; 4 of 4 parameters above 50. Strict > thresholds; no weighted average.

recommended_reason: Founder Score, Vision & Product above 75; 4 of 4 parameters above 50. Strict > thresholds; no weighted average.

project_summary: Automates granular performance-driver analysis for private-equity portfolio companies.
school_or_lab: MIT delta v / Martin Trust Center for MIT Entrepreneurship
institution_affiliation_basis: Named in the official MIT delta v 2025 Demo Day program.
source_locator: Official Demo Day 2025 program, PDF p. 27 (Torque)
funding_notes: Public-web screen through 2026-07-20 found no disclosed institutional equity round for this exact venture. MIT delta v participation may include equity-free fellowship support; this is not cap-table verification.
founder_score_rationale: Team-level execution signal: The team moved from proof of concept to a functioning product using real data from three customers. No university prestige or network proxy was scored. Individual contribution is not separately evidenced in the source.
vision_product_rationale: Automated driver analysis and dynamic dashboards address a high-cost, recurring PE portfolio workflow. Score reflects problem clarity, product coherence, and prototype/pilot maturity rather than pedigree.
differentiation_rationale: The vertical focus and driver decomposition are useful, though defensibility versus BI/FP&A platforms is not yet clear. Defensibility, freedom to operate, and reproducibility remain to be independently validated.

evaluation_scope: Project/team-level public evidence; scores repeat across co-founders and do not isolate individual contribution.
key_evidence: Proof of concept; functional MVP; real data from 3 customers; advanced LOI discussions with 2 PE firms.
counter_evidence: No paid ARR, deployment duration, decision impact, data-security review, or renewal evidence was located.
unknowns: Customer names; paid status; data onboarding cost; security; sales cycle; founder commitment; cap table.
next_diligence_action: Reference all five firms, verify paid usage and time savings, inspect data pipeline/security, and assess competitive switching costs.
recommended_reason: Founder Score, Vision & Product above 75; 4 of 4 parameters above 50. Strict > thresholds; no weighted average.

funding_notes: Public-web screen through 2026-07-20 found no disclosed institutional equity round for this exact venture. MIT delta v participation may include equity-free fellowship support; this is not cap-table verification.
founder_score_rationale: Team-level execution signal: The team translated industry access into a working collaboration product and obtained deposits and LOIs before launch. No university prestige or network proxy was scored. Individual contribution is not separately evidenced in the source.

unknowns: Ideal customer; pricing; workflow migration; competitive moat; conversion from LOI; cap table.
next_diligence_action: Verify deposit/LOI counterparties, observe live workflow adoption, test pricing, and map incumbents and switching costs.
recommended_reason: 4 of 4 parameters above 50. Strict > thresholds; no weighted average.

unknowns: Program reach; measured health outcomes; funding concentration; leadership roles; operating runway; governance.

No university prestige or network proxy was scored. Individual contribution is not separately evidenced in the source.
vision_product_rationale: The interface reduces a clear configuration barrier in robotics simulation and reinforcement learning. Score reflects problem clarity, product coherence, and prototype/pilot maturity rather than pedigree.

evaluation_scope: Project/team-level public evidence; scores repeat across co-founders and do not isolate individual contribution.
key_evidence: FastAPI/Next.js/MuJoCo stack; natural-language generation/refinement; browser simulation; ~67 FPS reported.
counter_evidence: Built during a hackathon; no verified incorporation, full-time commitment, customer retention, paid revenue, or cap table was located. No sim-to-real or external-user validation.
unknowns: Whether the team is continuing; founder role split; incorporation; IP ownership; customer discovery; pricing; cap table; current funding. Model reliability; template coverage; training pipeline; user demand.
next_diligence_action: Confirm which creators are continuing full time, inspect the repository/demo, run customer references, and obtain entity/cap-table/funding confirmation.

key_evidence: GitHub/Slack/document ingestion; pgvector/HNSW retrieval; cross-encoder reranking; MCP server; live reader/writer agents.
counter_evidence: Built during a hackathon; no verified incorporation, full-time commitment, customer retention, paid revenue, or cap table was located. Enterprise security and scale were not independently tested.
unknowns: Whether the team is continuing; founder role split; incorporation; IP ownership; customer discovery; pricing; cap table; current funding. Permissions; data isolation; latency; customer wedge; deployment model.

founder_score_rationale: Team-level execution signal: The team built multi-source ingestion, structured Markdown memory, vector/BM25 retrieval, reranking, graph visualization, live agents, and an MCP server. No university prestige or network proxy was scored. Individual contribution is not separately evidenced in the source.
vision_product_rationale: Shared inspectable memory addresses a real failure mode in enterprise agent deployments. Score reflects problem clarity, product coherence, and prototype/pilot maturity rather than pedigree.
differentiation_rationale: Bidirectional human/agent editing, graceful retrieval fallbacks, and MCP integration form a credible product wedge. Defensibility, freedom to operate, and reproducibility remain to be independently validated.
traction_rationale: A complete weekend artifact exists; no external company deployment, paid user, retention, or reliability benchmark was verified. Hackathon selection, accelerator admission, grants, and prizes are not counted as customer traction.
evaluation_scope: Project/team-level public evidence; scores repeat across co-founders and do not isolate individual contribution.
key_evidence: GitHub/Slack/document ingestion; pgvector/HNSW retrieval; cross-encoder reranking; MCP server; live reader/writer agents.
counter_evidence: Built during a hackathon; no verified incorporation, full-time commitment, customer retention, paid revenue, or cap table was located. Enterprise security and scale were not independently tested.
unknowns: Whether the team is continuing; founder role split; incorporation; IP ownership; customer discovery; pricing; cap table; current funding. Permissions; data isolation; latency; customer wedge; deployment model.
next_diligence_action: Security-review the architecture, reproduce ingestion/retrieval, identify a design partner, then confirm which creators are continuing full time, inspect the repository/demo, run customer references, and obtain entity/cap-table/funding confirmation.
recommended_reason: Founder Score, Vision & Product, Differentiation above 75; 3 of 4 parameters above 50. Strict > thresholds; no weighted average.
